title: Uniformity of c over the full range ε∈(0,1), especially ε→0 and εn=O(1)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap.** The constant $c$ must be independent of $G$, $|V|=n$, and $\varepsilon$. Concentration/sampling arguments typically degrade as the sample size $\sim\varepsilon n$ shrinks: relative fluctuations grow, so a bound proved for $\varepsilon$ bounded away from $0$ does not automatically extend.

Required:
1. Treat the regime $\varepsilon n = O(1)$ separately. Note a singleton $|S|=1$ is always $\varepsilon$-light ($L_S=0$), and more generally any independent set is $\varepsilon$-light for all $\varepsilon\ge0$; verify this settles small-$\varepsilon$ with the right $c$ (need $c\varepsilon n\le 1$ when only the singleton is available).
2. Show the main concentration/selection argument retains a *uniform* constant as $\varepsilon\to0$, or stitch the two regimes together with a single $c$.
3. Handle endpoints $\varepsilon=0$ (only edgeless $S$) and $\varepsilon=1$ ($S=V$) explicitly.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Uniformity of $c$ — detailed spec

$c$ must be chosen BEFORE $G$ and $\varepsilon$ and work across all $\varepsilon\in(0,1)$.
- **Regime $\varepsilon n=O(1)$ (incl. $\varepsilon\to0$):** if $c\varepsilon n\le1$, any singleton $S=\{u\}$ has $L_S=0\preceq\varepsilon L$, so the bound is met trivially. Handle this explicitly so the main argument only needs $\varepsilon n\gtrsim1$.
- **Regime $\varepsilon\to1$:** must still yield $|S|\ge cn$.
- **Forbidden:** a bound where $c$ secretly depends on $n$ (e.g. $1/\log n$) or on $\varepsilon$ (e.g. $c\sim\varepsilon$). Audit the final constant for hidden dependencies.
